title: Human Reports, Machine Output, Terminal Width, and Accessibility · Stakeholder Software Mission
summary: A low-bandwidth public-library analytics team. They need batch and streaming equivalence, a typed immutable pipeline, bounded memory, narrow human reports, stable JSON, separated diagnostics, terminal reflow, plain operation, and screen-reader-usable labels. Planned artifact: Deliver Bounded Accessible Report Pipeline with acceptance evidence, rubric defense, reflection, and a changed-constraint extension. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debu…
objectives: Compare batch and streaming results across adversarial split points, malformed data, and final fragments.
Model input identity, policy, token, statistics, ranking row, and report metadata with explicit dataclasses or typed records.
Version JSON or CSV report schemas, preserve types and ordering, and reject non-finite or ambiguous values.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-bookbot-python cumulative Unicode-safe text-analysis portfolio.

Browser execution uses original fixed in-memory fixtures and deterministic pure-Python models only.
Host files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, package installation, native profiling, load,
operating-system races, publication, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class TextPolicy:
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    normalization: str = "NFC"
    casefold: bool = True
    top_k: int = 10


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    corpus_id: str
    revision: str
    policy_version: str
    command_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Bookbot portfolio ready"

steps.title: Bookbot and Python Commit to the mission model · Project Batch Stream Equivalence
steps.instruction:
project case — A project Bookbot team handles case 2075 during an offline public-library reading report. Build deterministic Python evidence to format a reflowable report with separated diagnostics, no color-only meaning, and structured alternatives; test empty, malformed, Unicode, tied, large, changed-policy, and rejected cases before accepting output; then split one word and one UTF-8 sequence across stream chunks and compare batch output byte-for-byte. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

steps.title: Bookbot and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Project Structured Alternative
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: bookbot-structured-alternative-1v1rs0" that uses case 4910 to format a reflowable report with separated diagnostics, no color-only meaning, and structured alternatives. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not open host files, run processes or Git, install packages, read credentials or host state, publish artifacts, or cause external effects; verify native boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, Unicode 17, disposable files and environments, real CLI and package runs, profiling, accessibility, security, load, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

content.code:
# Evidence: bookbot-structured-alternative-1v1rs0
# Competency: bookbot-structured-alternative.
# Case 4910: a large-file migration rehearsal.
# Operating constraint: test empty, malformed, Unicode, tied, large, changed-policy, and rejected cases before accepting output.
# Changed case: introduce an equal-frequency tie and prove the secondary ranking key is stable.
def evidence_bookbot_structured_alternative_1v1rs0(report_rows: int = 8, width: int = 72, diagnostics_on_stderr: bool = True, color_only_signals: int = 0, structured_alternative: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1v1rs0 = "1v1rs0-0sr1v1-4910"
    assert evidence_variant_1v1rs0
    evidence_axes_1v1rs0 = (
        "corpus1v1rs0",
        "policy0sr1v1",
        "revision4910",
        "command1v1rs04910",
        "report0sr1v14910",
        "test49101v1rs0",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1v1rs0)) == 6
    if report_rows < 1 or width < 40:
        return False, report_rows, "report-shape"
    if not diagnostics_on_stderr:
        return False, report_rows, "stream-contract"
    if color_only_signals > 0:
        return False, color_only_signals, "color-only"
    if not structured_alternative:
        return False, report_rows, "missing-alternative"
    assert width >= 40
    return True, report_rows, "accessible-report"
